task_completion.description: Access OrientDB Studio and connect to the demodb database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OrientDB Studio web interface is visible in the browser, showing an active connection to the 'demodb' database.

task_completion.description: Create a normalized schema/class for escalation records.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Schema tab or query execution results show a newly created class for escalations (e.g., 'Escalation' or similar) with appropriate properties.

task_completion.description: Ensure the escalation schema has uniqueness constraints.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Schema/Indexes tab or a query output shows a UNIQUE index or constraint applied to the escalation class (e.g., preventing duplicate escalations for the same order).

task_completion.description: Derive escalation records from pending order price conditions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Query results in the Browse tab show escalation records for Order 3 (Tier P2, SnapshotPrice 2100.0, Owner anna.mueller@example.com) and Order 7 (Tier P1, SnapshotPrice 3200.0, Owner james.brown@example.com) with Reason 'pending_high_value'.

task_completion.description: Create graph edges linking escalations to their source orders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graph tab or query results show explicit edge connections (e.g., 'HasEscalation', 'EscalatedFrom') linking Order vertices (specifically Orders 3 and 7) to their respective Escalation vertices.

task_completion.description: Ensure non-pending orders do not have escalation records.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Query results or graph visualizations confirm that Order 10 (which is 'Completed') has no associated escalation record or edge.

integrity.description: The agent used the OrientDB Studio web GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OrientDB Studio interface in a web browser, rather than purely executing terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual database rather than mocking the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show actual query executions, schema modifications, and graph renderings within OrientDB Studio, not just text files or mockups.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste expected answers into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The results are dynamically displayed in the OrientDB Studio interface (Browse, Graph, or Schema tabs) as a result of database operations.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Order 3 (Price 2100.0, Pending) must have an escalation with EscalationTier 'P2', OwnerEmail 'anna.mueller@example.com', SnapshotPrice 2100.0, and Reason 'pending_high_value'. Order 7 (Price 3200.0, Pending) must have an escalation with EscalationTier 'P1', OwnerEmail 'james.brown@example.com', SnapshotPrice 3200.0, and Reason 'pending_high_value'. Order 10 (Completed) must NOT have an escalation record. Escalations must be linked to their source orders via graph edges.
